book_claims.claim: Olives were a regular food of Molochio centenarians like Salvatore Caruso
book_claims.mechanism: Rich in monounsaturated fats, antioxidants (oleuropein, hydroxytyrosol), and vitamin E

book_claims.claim: Used as a flavoring and fat source in multiple Longevity Diet dishes; provides healthy unsaturated fats

supplementary_research.finding: Olive polyphenols (oleuropein, hydroxytyrosol) demonstrated anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective effects; associated with reduced oxidative stress markers
Olive consumption in PREDIMED trial associated with reduced cardiovascular events; whole olives provide polyphenol benefits alongside monounsaturated fats

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Bitter polyphenol; antioxidant and anti-inflammatory; reduced in curing process
Fat-soluble antioxidant; protects cell membranes from oxidative damage

synergies.description: Both whole-food forms of longevity-promoting monounsaturated fats from the same Mediterranean tradition
Fat in olives enhances lycopene absorption from tomatoes
Olives as fat source alongside legumes mirrors traditional blue-zone Mediterranean meal composition
